news.title: Gilead cancels midstage HIV trial as FDA’s clinical hold remains intact
news.snippet: After the FDA placed two of its next-gen HIV drugs on clinical hold last June, Gilead Sciences has now terminated one of the affected trials.

news.title: Valneva CEO on Pfizer-partnered Lyme vaccine: Why a phase 3 miss doesn’t tell the whole story
news.snippet: In the world of clinical trials, missing the primary endpoint of a pivotal trial typically spells real trouble for a drug program. But for Valneva CEO Thomas Lingelbach, the regulatory future of the P

news.title: Drug combo treats multiple cancers with ‘paradox’ mutation in mice
news.snippet: A detailed look at a mysterious mutation present in many different cancer types has revealed new drug targets that, when hit with a combo of well-known compounds, led to tumor cell death and prolonged

news.title: Thousands of clinical trial sites impacted by Middle East conflict: report
news.snippet: Nearly 8,000 clinical trial sites have been affected by the joint strikes on Iran by the U.S. and Israel and Iran’s subsequent retaliation, according to a report from clinical data science company Phe

news.title: Alzheimer's-focused Korsana to use Cyclerion reverse merger to go public
news.snippet: After emerging from stealth last month with $175 million in funding, Korsana Biosciences has wasted no time making its way to the public markets.

news.title: Oric chases Pfizer into phase 3 hailing prostate cancer edge, but investors send stock down
news.snippet: Oric Pharmaceuticals has picked the phase 3 dose for its prostate cancer prospect, barreling into the pivotal program while outlining a claimed edge over Pfizer’s rival candidate.

news.title: ‘There isn't as much meat left to cut’: Biopharma layoffs maintain slowdown in Q1
news.snippet: Have we passed the peak of biotech layoffs? It’s too early to say for certain—but an intriguing story is playing out in the figures.

news.title: FDA extends review of Orca Bio’s novel cell therapy for blood cancers
news.snippet: The FDA has delayed its target decision date for Orca Bio’s blood cancer cell therapy candidate Orca-T by three months to July 6 after the company submitted additional CMC-related data.

news.title: Whoop raises $575M series G, Abbott comes on board amid hiring spree
news.snippet: Whoop is whooping it up with a massive $575 million raise amid a strong start to the year for the fitness wearable.

news.title: KBP hopes to revive troubled heart drug at center of deal fraud dispute with Novo Nordisk
news.snippet: KBP Biosciences is looking to reclaim ownership of the ill-fated heart drug ocedurenone from Novo Nordisk following a botched $1.3 billion deal and fraud allegations, Fierce Biotech has learned.

news.title: Iterum initiates wind-down after failure to offload antibiotic with sluggish sales
news.snippet: Irish antibiotic specialist Iterum Therapeutics has started winding down its operations, citing limited cash amid the expensive commercialization of its only approved product, Orlynvah.

news.title: Still reeling from FDA refusal, IO Biotech surrenders to bankruptcy
news.snippet: Half a year after being spurned by the FDA and following several rounds of deep layoffs, IO Biotech has decided to close its doors for good.

news.title: Obesity biotech Ambrosia scores $100M series B to take next-gen oral GLP-1 into the clinic
news.snippet: Ambrosia Biosciences is aiming to move the market beyond peptide-based obesity treatments and initiate a trial for its oral small-molecule GLP-1 therapy, backed by an oversubscribed $100 million serie

news.title: Fierce Biotech Layoff Tracker 2026: Lipella, IO shutter; Takeda plots 634 US job cuts
news.snippet: As always, if you know of layoffs occurring at a biotech, please reach out to the Fierce Biotech editorial team.

news.title: BMS links up with Faro to use AI to improve clinical trial protocols
news.snippet: Bristol Myers Squibb has tapped up Faro to use AI to improve the Big Pharma's clinical trial protocols.

news.title: Lilly answers Novo's GLP-1 pill with highly anticipated FDA nod for Foundayo
news.snippet: The FDA has approved Lilly’s GLP-1 receptor agonist pill as the first new molecular entity cleared under the regulator’s controversial Commissioner's National Priority Voucher program.

news.title: Regulatory tracker: Vertex secures broader FDA labels for cystic fibrosis meds Alyftrek, Trikafta
news.snippet: In this tracker, Fierce Pharma is recording the regulatory progress of in-market products, including expansions into key geographies and new indications. Some of these updates may not meet the bar for

news.title: Novo's Wegovy nets cardio nod from UK cost gatekeeper, adding 1M+ eligible patients
news.snippet: The National Institute for Health and Care Excellence, which serves as the U.K.’s drug price regulator, has given the green light to Wegovy (semaglutide) as a weekly injection to prevent heart attacks

news.title: ‘There isn't as much meat left to cut’: Biopharma layoffs maintain slowdown in Q1
news.snippet: Have we passed the peak of biotech layoffs? It’s too early to say for certain—but an intriguing story is playing out in the figures.

news.title: FDA extends review of Orca Bio’s novel cell therapy for blood cancers
news.snippet: The FDA has delayed its target decision date for Orca Bio’s blood cancer cell therapy candidate Orca-T by three months to July 6 after the company submitted additional CMC-related data.

news.title: Evotec hires exec with AI experience to lead rebooted commercial team
news.snippet: Evotec has appointed Ashiq Khan, Ph.D., as chief commercial officer, putting an executive with robotics and artificial intelligence experience in charge of a unit that is central to the company’s rece

news.title: #FierceMadness: Your Final Four revealed for the 2026 Drug Name Tournament—VOTE NOW
news.snippet: It’s time once again for Fierce Pharma Marketing’s annual #FierceMadness tournament. This time around, we're watching 64 newly minted drug names go head to head.

news.title: FDA flags serious liver injury cases, 8 deaths with ‘reasonable’ link to Amgen's Tavneos
news.snippet: The agency identified 76 cases of drug-induced liver injury in patients who used Amgen's Tavneos, including eight deaths.

news.title: Novo Nordisk cuts 400 roles at troubled Bloomington site
news.snippet: Amid a major corporate overhaul, Novo Nordisk is pruning the ranks at its Bloomington, Indiana, production facility, which contributes to the blockbuster GLP-1 medications Ozempic and Wegovy.

news.title: Iterum initiates wind-down after failure to offload antibiotic with sluggish sales
news.snippet: Irish antibiotic specialist Iterum Therapeutics has started winding down its operations, citing limited cash amid the expensive commercialization of its only approved product, Orlynvah.

news.title: API supplier BASF raises prices up to 20% in response to rising energy, raw material costs
news.snippet: BASF Pharma Solutions will raise its prices by up to 20% for its excipients and some of its active pharmaceutical ingredients, effective immediately.

news.title: Biogen, eyeing swift commercial tailwind, ponies up $5.6B for Apellis and its 2 approved meds
news.snippet: Biogen is bulking up in immunology—and paving the way for its future ambitions in kidney diseases—with an M&A play that immediately adds two ongoing launches to its marketed drugs portfolio.

news.title: Insulet hires Stryker vet, reinstating commercial chief role as C-suite overhaul continues
news.snippet: Insulet has reinstated the chief commercial officer position three years after eliminating the role to pursue more focused regional and local market execution.

news.title: BMS, Novartis, Gilead, Iovance dinged over biologics promos in rare spate of CBER untitled letters
news.snippet: Promotional materials for a handful of biologics have found their way into the FDA’s hot seat.

news.title: Lilly presses for UK deal that would see higher drug prices in exchange for resumed investments: FT
news.snippet: The company is looking to work out an arrangement with the U.K. government that would see higher drug prices in exchange for Lilly to continue its U.K. investments, according to a report in The Financ

news.title: STAT+: Makary marks one year at FDA with focus on achievements in speech to staff
news.snippet: The FDA commissioner praised staff and listed his accomplishments in a speech to workers.

news.title: STAT+: Government watchdog urges FDA to finalize guidance for advisory committee conflicts of interest
news.snippet: The FDA has often failed to share information on how it determines whether advisory committee members have financial conflicts of interest, according to a new review.

news.title: Eli Lilly’s obesity pill approved by FDA, setting up fierce competition with Novo Nordisk
news.snippet: Eli Lilly’s obesity pill was approved by the FDA, setting it up for fierce competition against Novo Nordisk’s new Wegovy pill.

news.title: STAT+: Insilico Medicine CEO on how best to use AI in drug development
news.snippet: In this edition of STAT's AI Prognosis: How to be a 'productive' biotech AI company, with Insilico Medicine CEO Alex Zhavoronkov, and more.

news.title: STAT+: Novo’s subscription model raises questions about pharma-telehealth tie-ups
news.snippet: A delayed FDA decision for Orca Bio, new questions about pharma-telehealth tie-ups, and more

news.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about FDA view on peptide compounding, Iran war hurting trial recruitment, and more
news.snippet: The FDA is moving toward allowing compounding pharmacies to produce injectable peptides that were banned over significant safety concerns

news.title: Supreme Court rules against conversion therapy ban
news.snippet: Fact-checking Jay Bhattacharya, why PCOS might get a new name, and more health news from Morning Rounds

news.title: STAT+: Can a psychedelic ‘coach’ make ketamine therapy even more effective?
news.snippet: Should ketamine be used strictly as a pharmacological intervention, or should psychotherapy be incorporated? A new study is trying to tackle this question.

news.snippet: Here is STAT’s biotech scorecard, our regular ledger of stock-moving biotech events, for the second quarter.
